evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent better-auth API surface (betterAuth, prismaAdapter, toNextJsHandler, nextCookies, createAuthClient, authClient.signIn.social/magicLink). However, magic link is configured under `email.magicLink` rather than as a plugin in the `plugins` array, which contradicts the playbook's own pitfall #2 noting MagicLink must be registered as a plugin. Also `npx auth@latest generate` looks like a hallucinated CLI (real is `npx @better-auth/cli`).
evidence.executability: Fenced code blocks parse syntactically. Issues: middleware.ts uses untyped `req` in TS without NextRequest type, getSessionCookie typically requires the request's cookies/headers (signature may differ), and the magic-link config placement may cause runtime endpoint 404s. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo). Likely 1-2 non-obvious fixes required.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: (a) Google OAuth acquisition steps with console URL, (b) install bash blocks, (c) .env template with exact var names matching code (BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, DATABASE_URL), (d) server code (lib/auth.ts, route handler, middleware), (e) frontend (sign-in page, dashboard), (f) test commands via Jest examples though no single explicit `npm test` runnable command block. Env vars listed match those referenced in code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple links to better-auth.com/docs (official) placed inline near relevant code blocks (Next.js integration, magic-link plugin, options). Also cites developers.google.com/identity (official Google). Some third-party (GitHub issues, StackOverflow, MSW, Jest) but these are appropriate supporting refs.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling — internally consistent with version-less but coherent API usage, minor inconsistency between magic-link config (email.magicLink) and the playbook's own pitfall about plugin registration drops correctness from 4 to 3.
